sections.question_synthetic: Does the Franchise Tax Board's failure to publish its Conflict of Interest Code in the California Administrative Register violate CAPA?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that if FTB sent the Code to the Office of Administrative Hearings for publication, it satisfied CAPA notice requirements regardless of actual publication. Even if it failed to send the Code, FTB may argue substantial compliance with CAPA if it followed other notice procedures and provided public participation opportunities. A court would likely not invalidate the Code for this technical procedural failure.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document is significantly degraded with OCR errors and missing text. Some sections are illegible or incomplete. The core question and conclusion sections were extractable but with moderate confidence due to text quality issues. Document references Government Code §11423(f) and case law including California Medical Assn. v. Lackner.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter analyzing whether the Franchise Tax Board's failure to publish its Conflict of Interest Code in the California Administrative Register violates CAPA. The FPPC concludes that FTB likely satisfied or substantially complied with notice requirements under CAPA.
